name: Casa de la Moneda
description: One of the oldest and most important colonial buildings in the Americas. It served as the royal mint during the Spanish colonial era and is now a world-class museum.

name: Cerro Rico
description: The iconic mountain that made Potosí famous worldwide. Known as the 'Mountain that eats men', it produced vast amounts of silver that funded the Spanish Empire for centuries.

name: Cathedral of Potosí
description: A magnificent baroque cathedral facing the main square. Built between 1809 and 1836, it features stunning colonial architecture and is one of the finest churches in Bolivia.

name: Plaza 10 de Noviembre
description: The vibrant heart of Potosí city. Surrounded by colonial buildings, the cathedral, and the city hall, it is the main gathering place for locals and tourists alike.

name: San Francisco Church
description: One of the oldest churches in Bolivia, dating back to 1547. It features remarkable colonial art and architecture, and its bell tower offers panoramic views of the city.

name: Mercado Artesanal
description: A colorful traditional market where local artisans sell handmade silver jewelry, woven textiles, and traditional Bolivian crafts. A must-visit for cultural souvenirs.

name: Kari Kari Lakes
description: A series of artificial lakes built by the Spanish colonizers in the 16th century to power the silver mills. Today they offer beautiful scenery and recreational activities.

name: Convento de Santa Teresa
description: A colonial convent founded in 1685, now converted into a fascinating museum. It houses an impressive collection of religious art, colonial furniture, and historical artifacts.
